entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: Pennisetum glaucum (L.) R.Br, the crop studied for establishment and yield under wind erosion conditions

entities.name: MILLET STOVER RESIDUE
entities.description: Crop residue applied at levels of 0, 500, and 2000 kg ha−1 to control wind erosion and stimulate growth

entities.name: ESTABLISHMENT
entities.description: The process of seedlings emerging and becoming established in the field

entities.name: GROWTH
entities.description: The physical development and biomass accumulation of the plant

entities.name: WIND EROSION
entities.description: The process of soil removal by wind that buries seedlings and reduces yields

entities.name: NIGER
entities.description: The country in West Africa where the research was conducted

entities.name: WEST AFRICA
entities.description: The region where the research was conducted

entities.name: BIOMASS YIELD
entities.description: The total biological mass produced by the crop

entities.name: GRAIN PRODUCTION
entities.description: The amount of grain harvested per unit area

entities.name: SAND STORMS
entities.description: Severe wind events that cause soil to blow and cover seedlings

entities.name: DRY MATTER
entities.description: Organic material left in the field to aid in erosion control

entities.name: WIND EROSION CONTROL
entities.description: The implementation of measures like crop residue to prevent wind erosion

relationships.description: Used as a measure to control
